trouble to both the ruler and the ruled (i.e. country).
^ nun
If fire is observed on water, flesh or anything wet, it foresha-
dows the king's death; if on weapons, a sanguinary war; and if the
fire goes out in military camps, villages or towns, there will be
danger from fire,
[After the invention of matches there is no question of fire
going out in modern times. But till about 60 years ago in many
Indian homes fire was never allowed to be extinguished. Should a
woman find that in her hearth the fire has gone out completely, she
would go to the neighbouring house and borrow a few embers.]
Should temples, houses, triumphal arches, flags and the like be
burnt without fire or by lightning, there would undoubtedly be an
attack by a foreign army after six months.

Portentous Phenomena XLVI 379
[The word snfe in is explained by Utpala as referring to
store-room, granary etc.]
Should there arise smoke without fire, and dust and darkness
